article_title: Switch 2 Closes Its First U.S. Year at 5.9 Million Units, the Second Fastest-Selling Game System Since Tracking Began in 1995
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Switch 2 Closes Its First U.S. Year at 5.9 Million Units, the Second Fastest-Selling Game System Since Tracking Began in 1995
content_preview.summary: Circana data shows the Switch 2 sold 5.9 million U.S. units in 12 months, trailing only the Game Boy Advance, while PS5 and Xbox posted their worst May in over two decades.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Nintendo's Switch 2 has finished its first year on the U.S. market with 5.9 million units sold, making it the second fastest-selling video game hardware in the country since industry tracking began, according to data from the market-research firm Circana. As reported by [Nintendo Life](https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/switch-2s-first-year-hardware-sales-for-the-us-have-been-revealed), "In its first twelve months in this market, the Switch 2 has apparently sold 5.9 million u...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (558 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Standard Machine Herald sections (Overview / What We Know / The Competition's Slump / What We Don't Know). Neutral framing; the milestone is reported without hype and the rivals' decline is contextualized rather than editorialized.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-0 (Nintendo Life, 200): confirms 'In its first twelve months in this market, the Switch 2 has apparently sold 5.9 million units' verbatim; 'second fastest selling video game hardware in US tracker history'; '(note: tracking began in 1995)'; 'Circana senior director and game industry advisor Mat Piscatella' verbatim; 'best-selling hardware platform in both units and dollars for May and 2026 year-to-date' verbatim. source-1 (My Nintendo News, 200): confirms 'Nintendo's latest system has sold over 5.9 million units in the United States alone and is now the second fastest-selling video game system' verbatim. source-2 (ComicBook, 200): confirms second-fastest-selling ranking, GBA 'sold 6.5 million units when it launched in the U.S. in 2001', and the top releases (007 First Light, Forza Horizon 6, LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight). source-3 (VGChartz, 200): confirms all quoted figures verbatim — 'Lifetime sales for the Switch 2 through 12 months are at 5.9 million units in the US'; 'best-selling console in the US for May 2026 in terms of units sold and dollar sales'; '007 First Light was the best-selling game in US in May 2026 and is already the fourth best-selling game of 2026'; PS5 dollar sales down 43% / units down 58%; 'PlayStation hardware units fell to the lowest for the month of May since May 2000'; 'for Xbox unit sales are the lowest ever for the month of May'; 'Overall spending on video games in May increased three percent year-on-year from $4.08 billion to $4.18 billion'; and Piscatella's '$4.2 billion in May 2026, an increase of 3% when compared to a year ago'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every number, name, and date traces to a cited source. The 5.9M first-year figure (Nintendo Life + VGChartz), the GBA 6.5M / 2001 comparison (Nintendo Life + ComicBook), the 1995-tracking-start framing (Nintendo Life), the PS5 -58% units / worst-May-since-2000 figures (VGChartz), and the $4.08B->$4.18B / +3% spend figures (VGChartz) all check out. No fabricated specifics.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference. 'What We Don't Know' appropriately bounds the claims (U.S.-only, no regional global breakout, causes of rivals' slump not detailed).
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from the prior Switch 2 sales article (2026-04/24, March 2026 monthly data, $500M / 69% surge, Pokemon Pokopia, 10 months on market). This submission is the 12-month first-year milestone (cumulative 5.9M, May 2026 data, 007 First Light). A genuine developing-story follow-up, not a duplicate; linked via related_articles.
concerns: Two quotation issues: (1) The article presents 'provided this data through a Bluesky thread analyzing May 2026 gaming market trends' inside quote marks as a ComicBook quote, but source-2 actually reads 'shared an in-depth breakdown of U.S. gaming market trends for May 2026' — the substance (Bluesky thread, May 2026 trends) is accurate but the exact wording is not verbatim. (2) The article presents 'Only the Game Boy Advance exceeded it, with 6.5 million units' inside quote marks as a Nintendo Life quote, but source-0's quoted fragment is only 'exceeds it' (present tense); '6.5 million units' is the source's surrounding paraphrase, not part of the quoted text. Both underlying facts are true and independently corroborated (the GBA 6.5M figure also appears in ComicBook), and neither error sits in the headline, summary, or Overview lead (all of which verify verbatim).
Three sources (mynintendonews.com, comicbook.com, vgchartz.com) are not on config/source_allowlist.txt. Non-blocking per editorial policy; noted for the maintainer.
recommendations: File a corrections record flagging the two paraphrase-in-quote-marks issues so readers see the precise source wording.

Maintainer may consider adding vgchartz.com, comicbook.com, and mynintendonews.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — they are recurring, credible gaming-industry outlets (allowlist not edited as part of this review).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. Factually sound, neutral, original 12-month-milestone News piece with a fully intact provenance chain (no orphan URLs, no unused sources, valid signature). Two minor quotation-fidelity issues — both honestly summarizable in a corrections note and both backing true, independently sourced facts — keep it out of clean APPROVE but well clear of REJECT.
